examples.title: Basic dropdown
examples.description: A button trigger with a list of menu items.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Dropdown } from '@lostgradient/cinder/dropdown';

</script>

<Dropdown id="dropdown">
  <Dropdown.Trigger>Options</Dropdown.Trigger>
  <Dropdown.Menu>
    <Dropdown.Item>Edit</Dropdown.Item>
    <Dropdown.Item>Duplicate</Dropdown.Item>
    <Dropdown.Item variant="danger">Delete</Dropdown.Item>
  </Dropdown.Menu>
</Dropdown>

examples.title: Placement
examples.description: Use bottom-start or bottom-end placement to align the menu with whichever trigger edge fits the surrounding layout.
examples.code: Bottom-start Aligned to the left edge Bottom-end Aligned to the right edge

examples.title: With clickable items
examples.description: Dropdown.Item accepts onclick handlers and a danger variant for destructive actions.
examples.code:
Document actions (selected = 'Copy link')}>Copy link (selected = 'Duplicate')}>Duplicate (selected = 'Delete')}>Delete

Selected: {selected}

examples.title: With group labels
examples.description: Dropdown.Group and Dropdown.Label expose accessible group boundaries for related menu actions.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Dropdown } from '@lostgradient/cinder/dropdown';
</script>

<Dropdown id="dropdown-with-label">
  <Dropdown.Trigger>Grouped menu</Dropdown.Trigger>
  <Dropdown.Menu>
    <Dropdown.Group labelledBy="dropdown-with-label-document">
      <Dropdown.Label id="dropdown-with-label-document">Document</Dropdown.Label>
      <Dropdown.Item>Rename</Dropdown.Item>
      <Dropdown.Item>Move</Dropdown.Item>
    </Dropdown.Group>
    <Dropdown.Group labelledBy="dropdown-with-label-sharing">
      <Dropdown.Label id="dropdown-with-label-sharing">Sharing</Dropdown.Label>
      <Dropdown.Item>Copy link</Dropdown.Item>
      <Dropdown.Item>Invite people</Dropdown.Item>
    </Dropdown.Group>
  </Dropdown.Menu>
</Dropdown>

examples.title: With separators
examples.description: Dropdown.Separator divides menu item groups; useful before destructive actions.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Dropdown } from '@lostgradient/cinder/dropdown';
</script>

<Dropdown id="dropdown-with-separator">
  <Dropdown.Trigger>More actions</Dropdown.Trigger>
  <Dropdown.Menu>
    <Dropdown.Item>Copy link</Dropdown.Item>
    <Dropdown.Item>Duplicate</Dropdown.Item>
    <Dropdown.Separator />
    <Dropdown.Item variant="danger">Delete</Dropdown.Item>
  </Dropdown.Menu>
</Dropdown>
